title: Concurrency, Shared State, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Load Shedding · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent service team receives a different API, load, trust, persistence, and failure constraint and must transfer concurrency, shared state, backpressure, rate limits, and load shedding into a new concurrency budget and overload decision model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of concurrency budget and overload decision model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Treat handlers as concurrent, protect shared state with ownership or synchronization, and verify with race tests under changed schedules.
Bound active expensive work with semaphores, worker pools, queue capacity, context-aware admission, fairness, and per-tenant partitions.
Choose trusted identity, token bucket or window semantics, burst, refill, distributed consistency, Retry-After, and bypass policy from abuse risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Handler Concurrency Race
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 5033 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine concurrency capacity with a separate tenant rate budget; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Handler Concurrency Race.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat handlers as concurrent, protect shared state with ownership or synchronization, and verify with race tests under changed schedules. For Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Handler Concurrency Race, keep Handler Concurrency Race connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Overload Observability Recovery happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Overload Observability Recovery for concurrency budget and overload decision model.

steps.title: Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Overload Observability Recovery
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Overload Observability Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab team handles case 5042 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine concurrency capacity with a separate tenant rate budget; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure active, queued, rejected, canceled, completed, saturated, and recovered work without high-cardinality labels and rehearse changed-load recovery.

Evidence target: Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Overload Observability Recovery.
steps.why: Retrieving Overload Observability Recovery through the concrete concurrency budget and overload decision model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Overload Observability Recovery, keep Overload Observability Recovery connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure active, queued, rejected, canceled, completed, saturated, and recovered work without high-cardinality labels and rehearse changed-load recovery.

hints: For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Overload Observability Recovery, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Overload Observability Recovery precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Overload Observability Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab team handles case 5042 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine concurrency capacity with a separate tenant rate budget; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure active, queued, rejected, canceled, completed, saturated, and recovered work without high-cardinality labels and rehearse changed-load recovery.

For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Overload Observability Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A low average latency proves the server has enough capacity during burst and tail conditions.
